pr.title: Cleanup Kirby's Char Copy table, start cleanup of Copy Costumes
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Cleaned up a large Kirby copy-related data region in asm/data.s by replacing raw hexadecimal string data and hard-coded address literals with labeled .asciz strings and symbolic .4byte references. The PR focused on Kirby's character-copy table and began similar cleanup for Kirby copy costume data, making data tables easier to read and safer to reference during later decompilation.

key_files.role: Converted Kirby copy/costume data from raw .4byte encodings and numeric addresses into explicit labels, .asciz strings, and symbolic pointers.

what_changed: Decoded many raw .4byte ASCII sequences into readable .asciz strings for Kirby copy asset names such as "PlKbCpCa.dat", "ftDataKirbyCopyCaptain", "PlKbCpDk.dat", "ftDataKirbyCopyDonkey", "PlKbCpGw.dat", and "ftDataKirbyCopyGamewatch".
Added global labels for previously implicit string locations, including labels in the lbl_803CA648 through lbl_803CA9B8 range for Kirby character-copy file names and ftDataKirbyCopy names.

Replaced hard-coded data pointers like .4byte 0x803CA648, .4byte 0x803CACB0, .4byte 0x803CAED0, .4byte 0x803CB0F0, .4byte 0x803CB310, and .4byte 0x803CB3A0 with symbolic references such as .4byte lbl_803CA648 and .4byte lbl_803CACB0.
Introduced labeled sub-tables for Kirby copy costume data, including lbl_803CACB0 for Donkey Kong copy costume entries, lbl_803CAED0, lbl_803CB0F0, lbl_803CB310, and lbl_803CB3A0.

Started decoding costume-specific Kirby copy asset strings, including examples like "PlKbNrCpDk.dat", "PlyKirbyDk_Share_joint", "PlyKirbyDk_Share_matanim_joint", color variants such as Ye/Bu/Re/Gr/Wh, and Game & Watch copy strings.
Added labels inside another data-table region around lbl_803D6558 and lbl_803D6858, then replaced numeric references in lbl_803D6878 with symbolic label references.
smart_moves: Kept the assembly data layout intact while improving readability: decoded strings were emitted with .asciz followed by .balign 4, preserving the alignment implied by the original .4byte representation.
Used symbolic labels in pointer tables instead of raw absolute addresses, making future table edits less brittle and easier to audit.
Split packed raw data into separately labeled string objects before referencing them from tables, clarifying where each asset name begins and ends.
Preserved NULL table entries while converting neighboring entries to labels, which helps maintain the original table shape and character slot alignment.
Made the Kirby copy data searchable by real asset names rather than only by hexadecimal encodings or anonymous lbl addresses.
decomp_lessons: When a data.s region contains ASCII encoded as .4byte values, decode it to .asciz only when the original byte layout and alignment can be preserved.
For asset-name tables, prefer a label-per-string plus a separate pointer table; this makes the table structure visible and reduces dependence on numeric addresses.
Hard-coded addresses in .4byte table entries should be converted to labels once the target location is identified.
Keep explicit NULL entries in data tables during cleanup, because they often encode slot gaps or absent variants and are part of the matching layout.
Large data cleanup can be staged: this PR completed much of Kirby's character-copy table and only began copy costume cleanup, leaving later work to continue decoding remaining raw strings.
naming_conventions: Kirby copy file names use the observed asset naming pattern PlKbCpXX.dat, e.g. PlKbCpCa.dat, PlKbCpDk.dat, PlKbCpKp.dat, PlKbCpGw.dat.

Kirby copy fighter data strings use ftDataKirbyCopy<Name>, with examples including ftDataKirbyCopyCaptain, ftDataKirbyCopyDonkey, ftDataKirbyCopyKoopa, ftDataKirbyCopyGamewatch, and ftDataKirbyCopyEmblem.
Copy costume files include color or variant codes between PlKb and Cp, such as PlKbNrCpDk.dat, PlKbYeCpDk.dat, PlKbBuCpDk.dat, PlKbReCpDk.dat, PlKbGrCpDk.dat, and PlKbWhCpDk.dat.
Kirby copy costume joint strings use PlyKirby<Copy><Color>_Share_joint and material animation strings use PlyKirby<Copy><Color>_Share_matanim_joint, as shown by Donkey Kong color variants.
The PR retained address-derived labels of the form lbl_803Cxxxx and lbl_803Dxxxx rather than introducing semantic C-style symbol names.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted hexadecimal ASCII .4byte sequences to .asciz strings with .balign 4 to keep word alignment stable.
Replaced absolute address literals in pointer tables with label references, e.g. .4byte 0x803CA648 became .4byte lbl_803CA648.
Introduced .global directives for data labels that are referenced by later pointer tables.
Separated contiguous string blobs from pointer arrays by adding labels at the exact original addresses.
Preserved table cardinality and NULL padding while changing only representation from raw addresses/bytes to labels/strings.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that continue cleanup of Kirby copy costume strings after lbl_803CACF8.
Search for remaining .4byte hexadecimal ASCII in asm/data.s near Kirby copy costume regions.
Look for later semantic renames of lbl_803CA9D0, lbl_803CACB0, lbl_803CB3E8, or related Kirby copy pointer tables.
Check whether PlKbCpXX.dat and ftDataKirbyCopy<Name> tables were eventually moved from asm/data.s into typed C data.
